prerequisites: An Amplitude account with at least one project that has collected event data.
Manager or Admin role on the Amplitude organization, since only Managers and Admins can generate and view API keys and secret keys.
The project's API Key and Secret Key (api_key and secret_key), copied at the time the secret key is generated because Amplitude does not store or allow retrieval of a secret key afterward.
Knowledge of your project's data region (data_region), since US and EU projects use different API hosts.
A start_date marking the earliest date from which to begin syncing data.

authMethods.name: API Key and Secret Key (HTTP Basic Authentication)
authMethods.summary: Amplitude's Dashboard and Export REST APIs authenticate with the project's API Key and Secret Key sent as HTTP Basic Auth credentials (API Key as the username and Secret Key as the password). Supply both the api_key and secret_key fields from your Amplitude project. These are the only credentials this connector accepts.

setupSteps.title: Open your Amplitude project credentials
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Amplitude web app as a Manager or Admin. Open Organization settings from the upper navigation, then select API Keys in the side navigation and choose the project you want to sync.

setupSteps.title: Generate and copy the API Key and Secret Key
setupSteps.body: Copy the project's API Key. If you do not already have a Secret Key, generate one. Amplitude shows the secret key only at generation time and cannot retrieve it later, so copy and store it securely immediately. Use these values for the connector's api_key and secret_key fields.

setupSteps.title: Set the data region
setupSteps.body: Set data_region to match where your Amplitude project stores data. US projects use the api2.amplitude.com / amplitude.com hosts, and EU projects use the analytics.eu.amplitude.com host. Choosing the wrong region will cause authentication or data retrieval to fail.

setupSteps.title: Configure sync window and optional settings
setupSteps.body: Set the required start_date to the earliest date to sync from. Optionally configure request_time_range to control the size of each request window, and enable active_users_group_by_country if you want active-user metrics broken down by country.
